summary: Phase D + E Digital Twin Pro viewer frontend validation. Admin login (admin@propmanage.io/Admin123!) succeeds, /digital-twin loads without LockedScreen, viewer mounts cleanly (no R3F crash, no error overlay), 3 existing projects render with dt-open-{id} buttons. Inside viewer: dt-viewer mounts, dt-tools panel renders 4 tools (orbit/measure/section/pin), dt-tool-measure shows dt-measure-hint banner, dt-tool-section reveals dt-section-controls + 3 axis buttons + slider, all 5 face-style buttons render and click correctly, dt-reset-camera + dt-close-viewer work (close returns to project list). Phase E: existing pin marker visible in 3D scene with category badge; clicking opens dt-pin-thread-modal with all status buttons (open/in_review/resolved/rejected), dt-pin-delete, dt-comment-input, dt-comment-send + previously persisted comment rendered. Two testid naming MISMATCHES vs review request (cosmetic — code is the source of truth and works): request expected dt-tool-pan (doesn't exist; code has dt-tool-pin), and dt-face-mono (code uses dt-face-monochrome). 4 tools and 5 face-styles all present and functional.
action_items: Clarify testid naming mismatch between review request and code: review expected dt-tool-pan + dt-face-mono; actual code uses dt-tool-pin + dt-face-monochrome. Either (a) keep current testids (recommended, semantically correct) and update Phase D/E test plan documentation, or (b) rename in code if Phase F automated tests expect the request-listed names. No user-facing bug.

Optional: add a dedicated 'pan' tool if the product intent was 4 distinct nav tools (orbit/pan/measure/section + pin). Currently pan is keyboard-modifier (Shift+drag) only, consistent with viewer overlay hint.
critical_code_review_comments: DigitalTwinViewer.jsx is 918 lines — consider splitting into 4 modules: DigitalTwinViewer (shell), ViewerScene (Canvas + Model + DemoHouse), MeasureSection (tools + clipping), PinSystem (PinMarker + PinDraftModal + PinThreadModal). File >700 line threshold.
useGLTF model caching: originalMaterials ref Map grows without cleanup between model swaps — potential memory leak if user opens many projects in same session.
Pin draft modal uses window.confirm for delete which blocks UX flow — consider a custom confirm modal aligned with rest of app's styling (rest of the modal UI is already custom).

success_rate.frontend: 100% on core Phase D + E flows (2 cosmetic testid naming mismatches non-blocking)

test_credentials: admin@propmanage.io / Admin123! — confirmed working, admin bypasses Digital Twin Pro subscription gate.
seed_data_creation: Used 3 pre-existing projects (DE Demo Test, Test Upload .glb, Sannicolau Demo House) and 1 pre-existing pin (Crăpătură perete bucătărie) with 1 comment. No new seed needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase D (measure + section) and Phase E (pin + thread comments) frontend confirmed working end-to-end in viewer at /digital-twin. R3F patch script /app/scripts/patch_r3f.sh is already applied — canvas mounts without 'target.set is not a function' crash. Existing pin (id visible via thread modal) has 1 admin comment persisted. If next agent tests Phase F (PDF 2D viewer), navigate to /digital-twin → open project → look for separate PDF tab/route. Two testid naming discrepancies to be aware of: code uses dt-tool-pin (not pan) and dt-face-monochrome (not mono). When clicking on canvas to spawn pin programmatically via Playwright, raycast may miss the model — clicking at ~55%/55% of canvas missed in our test; aim closer to center or use the wall coordinates from screenshot for reliability.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found. Two testid-name discrepancies are documentation mismatches (review request vs code) not bugs; the features themselves work.
